_note: Original, owned NET board messages — our prose, era of Mona Lisa Overdrive.

messages.body: The gray sphere is real, it is not on any index, and the loa will not discuss it, which for the loa is practically a press release. Whatever's being built in there, the gods are treating it like family business.
Your handle's dues are paid through forever, cowboy, wherever you're logged in from these days. The board keeps your seat. First round's on the house when you visit.

Confirmed by three sources: a certain mirror-eyed contractor has closed her last account and walked. No forwarding address, no unfinished business — and for the first time in living memory, nobody holding her paper. Drinks were raised. Nobody was dumb enough to toast her by name.

Kid: paper filed, ghost voided, your host's townhouse career concluded per estate planning. Your old man's war is over an' the winners want their daughter back. Go home proud — you did the family trade credit. — F.

Open call: the network seeks fresh faces for upcoming flagship productions. Applicants may be surprised what the cameras already love about them. Inquire within. Continuity thanks you for your interest.

messages.to: GRAY SPHERE, C/O THE HONEYMOON SUITE
messages.body: Wedding cleanup's done. Robots fine. Cherry stayed. Gentry says tell you the Shape was two people all along and he's 'revising the monograph.' Porch light looks good from out here. — S.H.
